title: Derived nodes return undefined/None without subscriber
symptom: get() returns undefined/None on a derived node
cause: Derived nodes are lazy — no subscriber means no computation (spec §2.2)
fix: Add keepalive subscription inside factory: node.subscribe(() => {})
diagnostic: Check node.status — 'disconnected' means no subscriber, 'errored' means fn threw

title: Subscribe to outputs before emitting to inputs
symptom: Observer misses messages that were emitted before subscription
cause: Reactive systems deliver to current subscribers only; late subscribers miss past emissions
fix: Wire sinks/observers before sources/emitters. In factories, wire topology in reverse order
escape_hatch: TopicGraph.retained() returns buffered entries — SubscriptionGraph does this automatically

title: Guard null/undefined in effect fns
symptom: Effect fn throws or silently processes null when deps hold initial null value
cause: Effect nodes fire on activation with current dep values, including null/undefined initials
fix: Add 'if (val == null) return;' at the top of every effect fn that processes structured data

title: ReactiveMapBundle wraps values in Versioned
symptom: Accessing map values requires navigating .value.map (TS) or .value (PY) through Versioned wrapper
cause: Versioned wrapper exists for efficient RESOLVED dedup (compare version numbers, not deep equality)
fix: Use bundle.get(key) for single-key reads. Only navigate Versioned when using the node as a reactive dep

title: Wire graph factories in sink-to-source order
pattern: 1. Create sinks (TopicGraphs, state nodes) → 2. Create processors (derived/effect) → 3. Keepalive internal nodes → 4. Mount subgraphs → 5. Return controller
reason: Ensures that when stage N emits, stage N+1 is already wired to receive

title: ReactiveMapBundle API differs between TS and PY
detail: TS has .get(key), .has(key), .size. PY has .set()/.delete()/.clear() but no .get(key). Tracked in docs/optimizations.md
fix: Check language-specific API. PY parity fix pending.

title: TopicGraph.retained() is the catch-up mechanism for late subscribers
detail: retained() returns all buffered entries. Does NOT violate design invariants: it's a synchronous read of existing graph state (same as node.get()), not polling (§5.8) or imperative trigger (§5.9)

title: Domain builders return Graph objects
detail: pipeline(), jobQueue(), agentMemory() all return Graph. Builder provides ergonomic construction; Graph provides uniform introspection (.describe()), lifecycle (.signal(), .destroy()), persistence (.snapshot()), and composition (.mount())

title: LLM cost control
pattern: state (knob via meta) → derived chain → gauges via meta

title: Security policy enforcement
pattern: state + derived + effect with PAUSE propagation

title: Human-in-the-loop
pattern: Two state nodes (human + LLM) → derived gate → effect

title: Custom domain signals
pattern: User-defined message types + onMessage to intercept; unhandled types forward through graph
